title: CIL Civil — Full-length Mock 07
pattern: CIL MT — 200 MCQ · 2 papers · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: As per Income Tax Department data (June 2026), India's net direct tax collections in early FY27 rose by about what percentage year-on-year?
questions.solution: Net direct tax collections rose ~14.6% YoY to about Rs 5.21 trillion. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, which country became the first Central Asian member of the New Development Bank (NDB)?
questions.solution: Uzbekistan became the first Central Asian nation to join the BRICS-backed NDB. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which India-born theoretical physicist won the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics?
questions.solution: Rajasthan-born physicist Jainendra K. Jain received the 2025 Wolf Prize in Physics. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which institution ranked first in the QS World University Rankings 2027 (released June 2026)?
questions.solution: Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT) ranked first in QS World University Rankings 2027. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In June 2026, which Indian jurist was elected a Judge of the International Tribunal for the Law of the Sea (ITLOS)?
questions.solution: Indian jurist Bimal N. Patel was elected to ITLOS in June 2026. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, PM Narendra Modi received the highest national award of which country?
questions.solution: PM Modi was conferred Slovakia's highest national award in 2026. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026, in which an Indian Army contingent participated, was held in which country?
questions.solution: The multinational Exercise KHAAN QUEST 2026 was held in Mongolia. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Who was named the new Chairman of GIC Re in June 2026?
questions.solution: The government named Hitesh Joshi as the new Chairman of GIC Re. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: In 2026, ONGC was assigned to develop a new strategic petroleum reserve (SPR) facility at which location?
questions.solution: ONGC was tasked with building a new underground crude oil SPR cavern at Mangaluru, Karnataka. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The India\u2013UK CETA and Social Security Agreement were reported to take effect from which date?
questions.solution: Reports indicated the India\u2013UK CETA would take effect from July 15, 2026. (Verify before publish.)

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The largest coalfield in India by reserves is the:
questions.solution: Jharia in Jharkhand holds major prime coking coal reserves.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Indian state has the longest coastline?
questions.solution: Gujarat has the longest coastline among Indian states.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The headquarters of Coal India Limited (CIL) is located in which city?
questions.solution: Coal India Limited is headquartered in Kolkata, West Bengal.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest coal-producing state in India in recent years?
questions.solution: Chhattisgarh has been India's largest coal-producing state, ahead of Odisha and Jharkhand.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Coal India Limited operates under which Union Ministry?
questions.solution: CIL functions under the Ministry of Coal, Government of India.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which subsidiary of CIL is the single largest coal-producing company?
questions.solution: South Eastern Coalfields Limited (SECL) is among CIL's largest producing subsidiaries.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Australia?
questions.solution: Canberra is the capital of Australia, not Sydney or Melbourne.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the capital of Canada?
questions.solution: Ottawa is the national capital of Canada.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Mount Kilimanjaro, the highest mountain in Africa, is located in which country?
questions.solution: Mount Kilimanjaro lies in north-eastern Tanzania.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which is the largest planet in the Solar System?
questions.solution: Jupiter is the largest planet by both mass and diameter.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which planet is known as the Red Planet?
questions.solution: Iron oxide on its surface gives Mars a reddish appearance.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The SI unit of electric current is the:
questions.solution: The ampere (A) is the SI base unit of electric current.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: Which gas is most abundant in Earth's atmosphere?
questions.solution: Nitrogen makes up about 78% of the atmosphere by volume.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: The powerhouse of the cell is the:
questions.solution: Mitochondria generate most of the cell's ATP, earning the 'powerhouse' name.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General Awareness
questions.stem: What is the chemical symbol of Gold?
questions.solution: Gold's symbol is Au (from Latin 'aurum'); Ag is silver.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: What is the average of the first 20 natural numbers?
questions.solution: Average of first n naturals = (n+1)/2 = (20+1)/2 = 10.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The average age of 5 persons is 28 years. If a person aged 38 leaves, the average of the remaining 4 is:
questions.solution: Total = 140; remaining = 140 − 38 = 102; average = 25.5.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A sum doubles itself in 10 years under simple interest. The annual rate is:
questions.solution: To double, SI = P, so rate = 100/T = 100/10 = 10%.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: 3 kg of rice at Rs 30/kg is mixed with 5 kg at Rs 80/kg. The average price per kg is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Compound interest on Rs 10000 at 12% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 30% of a number is 50, the number is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The HCF of 45 and 75 is:
questions.solution: The highest common factor of 45 and 75 is 15.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A car covers a distance at 60 km/h in 6 hours. The distance is:
questions.solution: Distance = speed × time = 60 × 6 = 360 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An item bought for Rs 800 is sold for Rs 960. The profit percentage is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Successive discounts of 15% and 20% on a marked price of Rs 1000 give a final price of:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The percentage increase when a value rises from 60 to 80 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: An article sold at Rs 300 incurs a 25% loss. Its cost price is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The LCM of 8 and 12 is:
questions.solution: The lowest common multiple of 8 and 12 is 24.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A number divided by 8 gives quotient 12 and remainder 4. The number is:
questions.solution: Number = divisor × quotient + remainder = 8×12+4 = 100.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The square root of 3025 is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 15 men complete a work in 15 days, 6 men will complete it in:
questions.solution: Men-days constant: 15×15 = 6×D, D = 37.5 days.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A 200 m long train crosses a pole in 12 seconds. Its speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A can do a work in 18 days and B in 30 days. Working together they finish it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A and B invest in the ratio 3 : 5. Out of a total profit of Rs 8000, A's share is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: A man goes at 40 km/h and returns over the same route at 20 km/h. His average speed is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The compound ratio of 3 : 5 and 4 : 7 is:
questions.solution: Compound ratio = (3×4) : (5×7) = 12 : 35.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Simple interest on Rs 4000 at 8% per annum for 2 years is:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: If 72 is divided in the ratio 2 : 7, the larger part is:
questions.solution: Larger part = 72 × 7/9 = 56.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in 4 h and pipe B in 15 h. Together they fill it in:

questions.subject: Paper-I · Numerical Ability
questions.stem: The probability of getting a prime number on a single throw of a fair die is:
questions.solution: Count the favourable outcomes out of 6 equally likely faces to get 1/2.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 5:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30\u00b0; at 5:00 the hands are 5 \u00d7 30 = 150\u00b0 apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of DAB is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of D, A, B to get 7.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which of these fractions is the largest?
questions.solution: Comparing decimal values, 4/5 is the largest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 2, 7, 13, 15?
questions.solution: 2, 7, 13 are prime; 15 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If each letter is replaced by the next letter, ROSE is coded as:
questions.solution: Shift each letter forward by one to get SPTF.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If 1 January is a Friday, what day is 8 January?
questions.solution: 8 \u2212 1 = 7 days, exactly one week, so it is again Friday.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: M, N, O, P, ?
questions.solution: Consecutive alphabet letters; after P comes Q.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: Copper, Iron, Oxygen, Zinc?
questions.solution: Oxygen belongs to a different category from the rest.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: DE, GH, JK, ?
questions.solution: Each pair skips one letter; after JK comes MN.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman said, 'His mother is the only daughter of my mother.' How is the woman related to the man?
questions.solution: The only daughter of her mother is the woman herself, so she is the man's mother.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: 2 : 4 :: 5 : ? (pattern n : n^2)

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 3, 6, 12, 21, ?
questions.solution: Differences grow by 3; adding the next difference gives 33.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 64, 125, 200, 216?
questions.solution: The others are perfect cubes; 200 is not.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Bird : Nest :: Bee : ?
questions.solution: Just as Nest relates to Bird, Hive relates to Bee.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A man walks 5 km north, then 12 km east. His distance from the start is:
questions.solution: Resultant = \u221a(5\u00b2 + 12\u00b2) = 13 km.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 1, 4, 9, 16, ?
questions.solution: These are perfect squares; next = 5\u00b2 = 25.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: In a row of 18 people, X is 8th from the left. X's position from the right is:
questions.solution: Position from right = 18 \u2212 8 + 1 = 11.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: 2, 6, 18, 54, ?
questions.solution: Each term is multiplied by 3, so next = 54 \u00d7 3 = 162.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: All roses are flowers. All flowers fade. Which conclusion follows?
questions.solution: Both premises are universal affirmatives, so the chained conclusion 'All roses fade' follows.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Find the next term: X, V, T, R, ?
questions.solution: Letters decrease by 2 each time, so the next letter is P.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.solution: Each number is halved, so 20 \u2192 10.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: A person facing east turns 90\u00b0 to the right. Now they face:
questions.solution: Turning right from east leads to South.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: The angle between the hands of a clock at 2:00 is:
questions.solution: Each hour = 30\u00b0; at 2:00 the hands are 2 \u00d7 30 = 60\u00b0 apart.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: If A=1, B=2, ... then the sum of the letters of BAD is:
questions.solution: Add the alphabet positions of B, A, D to get 7.

questions.subject: Paper-I · Reasoning
questions.stem: Which is the odd one out: 3, 5, 9, 11?
questions.solution: 3, 5, 11 are prime; 9 is composite.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the antonym of 'Optimist'.
questions.solution: An optimist's opposite is a 'pessimist'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Choose the correctly spelt word.
questions.solution: The correct spelling is 'Occasion' (one c, two s).

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She is good ___ mathematics.'
questions.solution: The idiom is 'good at' a subject or skill.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He has been living here ___ 2010.'
questions.solution: 'Since' is used with a point of time like a year.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Divide this ___ two parts.'
questions.solution: 'Divide into' is the correct collocation for separation.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is married ___ a doctor.'
questions.solution: The correct collocation is 'married to' someone.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'The book is ___ the table.'
questions.solution: 'On' indicates resting on a surface.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a person who cannot read or write':
questions.solution: Such a person is 'illiterate'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The study of living organisms is called:
questions.solution: 'Biology' is the study of all living organisms.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One who studies stars and celestial bodies is an:
questions.solution: An 'astronomer' scientifically studies celestial bodies.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: One word for 'a speech made without preparation':
questions.solution: An unprepared speech is delivered 'extempore'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: A place where birds are kept is called:
questions.solution: An 'aviary' houses birds; an apiary keeps bees.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to break the ice' means:
questions.solution: 'Break the ice' means to initiate conversation in a tense or new setting.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'a piece of cake' means:
questions.solution: 'A piece of cake' means something very easy to do.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'once in a blue moon' means:
questions.solution: 'Once in a blue moon' means something happening very rarely.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to bury the hatchet' means:
questions.solution: 'Bury the hatchet' means to end a quarrel and make peace.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The idiom 'to let the cat out of the bag' means:
questions.solution: It means to accidentally reveal a secret.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Child' is:
questions.solution: 'Child' has the irregular plural 'children'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: The plural of 'Mouse' is:
questions.solution: 'Mouse' has the irregular plural 'mice'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Neither he nor I ___ wrong.'
questions.solution: With 'neither...nor', the verb agrees with the nearer subject 'I', so 'am'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'Each of the students ___ a book.'
questions.solution: 'Each' is singular, so it takes 'has'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'He is ___ honest man.'
questions.solution: 'Honest' starts with a vowel sound, so 'an' is used.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Fill in the blank: 'She plays ___ violin beautifully.'
questions.solution: Musical instruments take the definite article 'the'.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'John wrote the letter.' is:
options: The letter is written by John
The letter was written by John
questions.solution: Past simple active becomes 'was written' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-I · General English
questions.stem: Passive voice of 'She is reading a book.' is:
options: A book was read by her
A book has been read by her
A book is being read by her
A book is read by her
questions.solution: Present continuous active becomes 'is being read' in the passive.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Tacheometry is mainly used to determine:
options: Horizontal distances and elevations without chaining
questions.solution: Tacheometry measures distances and elevations rapidly using stadia readings.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The number of links in a 30 m metric chain is:
questions.solution: A 30 m metric chain has 150 links of 0.2 m each.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.75, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The error due to the curvature of the earth in levelling is:
questions.solution: Earth's curvature makes distant points read lower; correction is +0.0785 d\u00b2 m.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 550 mm, clear cover 20 mm and 25 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Camber on a road surface is provided mainly for:
questions.solution: Camber lets rainwater drain off the carriageway to the sides.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Superelevation is provided on:
questions.solution: Superelevation counters centrifugal force on horizontal curves.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 2 m\u00b2 with velocity 4 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The CBR test is used in the design of:
questions.solution: California Bearing Ratio is used to design flexible pavement thickness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam with a central point load W over span L, the magnitude of the fixed-end moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a central point load is WL/8 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Rigid pavements are generally made of:
questions.solution: Rigid pavements use a cement concrete slab that resists loads in flexure.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Stopping sight distance depends on the driver's reaction time and the:
questions.solution: SSD = lag (reaction) distance + braking distance.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 100 mm wide and 400 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The broad gauge of Indian Railways measures:
questions.solution: Indian Broad Gauge is 1676 mm; 1435 mm is standard gauge.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The superelevation for a design speed of 80 km/h and radius 150 m (e = V\u00b2/127R) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Marshall stability test is used for:
questions.solution: The Marshall test determines optimum bitumen content in bituminous mixes.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Traffic volume is expressed as:
options: Number of vehicles per unit time
questions.solution: Traffic volume is the number of vehicles crossing a section per unit time.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 4 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shape of the ideal transition curve used on highways is a:
questions.solution: An ideal transition curve is a spiral where radius varies inversely with length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 100 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 1500 mm\u00b2. The direct stress is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Biochemical Oxygen Demand (BOD) indicates:
questions.solution: BOD measures the oxygen needed to biodegrade organic matter, indicating organic pollution.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The permissible pH range of drinking water as per IS 10500 is:
questions.solution: IS 10500 specifies a desirable pH of 6.5 to 8.5 for drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a fixed beam carrying a UDL w over span L, the support (fixed-end) moment is:
questions.solution: Fixed-end moment for a UDL is wL\u00b2/12 at each support.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The average domestic per-capita water demand in Indian cities is taken as about:
questions.solution: A domestic demand of about 135 lpcd is commonly assumed.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The thermal stress developed in a fully restrained bar for a temperature rise \u0394T is:
questions.solution: Restrained thermal stress = \u03b1E\u0394T, independent of length and area.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The coagulant most commonly used in water treatment is:
questions.solution: Alum (aluminium sulphate) is the usual coagulant for removing fine suspended matter.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Disinfection of municipal water supplies is most commonly achieved by:
questions.solution: Chlorination is the standard method for disinfecting drinking water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 200 m\u00b3 treating a flow of 100 m\u00b3/h is:
questions.solution: Detention time = Volume / Discharge = 200 / 100 = 2 hours.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Hardness in water is caused mainly by salts of:
questions.solution: Dissolved calcium and magnesium salts are the main cause of hardness.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 100 m. With back sight 1.5 m and fore sight 0.8 m, the RL of the next point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The activated sludge process is used for the treatment of:
questions.solution: The activated sludge process is an aerobic biological treatment for sewage.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Sedimentation tanks in water treatment primarily remove:
questions.solution: Sedimentation removes settleable suspended solids by gravity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries a central point load of 16 kN. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM = WL/4 = 16 \u00d7 5 / 4 = 20 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The most common index organism for faecal contamination of water is:
questions.solution: E. coli is used as the indicator of faecal pollution in water.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 40% and PL = 20%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The initial setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not be less than:
questions.solution: IS specifies a minimum initial setting time of 30 minutes for OPC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slump test on fresh concrete measures its:
questions.solution: The slump test is a measure of the workability (consistency) of fresh concrete.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If the fore bearing of a line is 45\u00b0, its back bearing is:
questions.solution: Back bearing = fore bearing \u00b1 180\u00b0 = 225\u00b0.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The actual size of a standard modular brick (without mortar) is:
questions.solution: The actual modular brick size is 190 \u00d7 90 \u00d7 90 mm; nominal size with mortar is 200 \u00d7 100 \u00d7 100 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 210 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.2, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The final setting time of ordinary Portland cement should not exceed:
questions.solution: IS limits the final setting time of OPC to 600 minutes (10 hours).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The water-cement ratio law was proposed by:
questions.solution: Abrams' law relates concrete strength inversely to the water-cement ratio.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure at a depth of 8 m in water (\u03c1 = 1000 kg/m\u00b3, g = 9.81 m/s\u00b2) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The compound mainly responsible for early strength gain in cement is:
questions.solution: C3S hydrates rapidly and gives early strength to cement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 2.5 m long carries a 6 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W \u00d7 L = 6 \u00d7 2.5 = 15 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Curing of concrete is done primarily to:
questions.solution: Curing maintains moisture so cement hydration continues and concrete gains strength.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a beam section subjected to bending, the bending stress is zero at the:
questions.solution: Bending stress varies linearly and is zero at the neutral axis (centroidal axis).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka for a soil with \u03c6 = 30\u00b0 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Poisson's ratio for structural steel is approximately:
questions.solution: Steel has a Poisson's ratio of about 0.3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 10 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 5 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The flexure (bending) equation is:
questions.solution: The bending equation relates moment, stress and curvature as M/I = \u03c3/y = E/R.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A point of contraflexure in a beam is where:
questions.solution: At a point of contraflexure the bending moment is zero and reverses sign.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 12 mm diameter bar is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The slenderness ratio of a column is defined as:
questions.solution: Slenderness ratio = effective length / least radius of gyration.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.4, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Euler's critical buckling load of a column is proportional to:
questions.solution: Pcr = \u03c0\u00b2EI/Le\u00b2, so it varies inversely with the square of effective length.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The shear stress distribution across a rectangular beam section is:
questions.solution: Transverse shear stress varies parabolically, peaking at the neutral axis.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 600 mm, clear cover 25 mm and 16 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The ratio of maximum to average shear stress for a rectangular section is:
questions.solution: For a rectangle, maximum shear stress is 1.5 times the average.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The discharge through a section of area 1.5 m\u00b2 with velocity 5 m/s is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Torsional shear stress in a circular shaft is given by:
questions.solution: The torsion formula is \u03c4/r = T/J = G\u03b8/L.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The strain energy stored per unit volume under axial stress \u03c3 is:
questions.solution: Resilience (strain energy density) = \u03c3\u00b2/2E.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The section modulus of a rectangular section 250 mm wide and 400 mm deep is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a simply supported beam is:
questions.solution: A simply supported beam is statically determinate (indeterminacy = 0).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The velocity head corresponding to a velocity of 3 m/s is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The degree of static indeterminacy of a propped cantilever beam is:
questions.solution: A propped cantilever has one redundant reaction, so indeterminacy = 1.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The carry-over factor for a prismatic member in moment distribution is:
questions.solution: For a prismatic member with a far fixed end the carry-over factor is 1/2.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: An axial load of 80 kN acts on a bar of cross-sectional area 1500 mm\u00b2. The direct stress is:
questions.solution: Stress = P/A = 80000 N / 1500 mm\u00b2 = 53.33 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The sum of distribution factors at a rigid joint equals:
questions.solution: Distribution factors at any joint always sum to unity.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The detention time of a tank of volume 150 m\u00b3 treating a flow of 100 m\u00b3/h is:
questions.solution: Detention time = Volume / Discharge = 150 / 100 = 1.5 hours.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The moment distribution method was developed by:
questions.solution: Hardy Cross developed the moment distribution method in 1930.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A three-hinged arch is:
questions.solution: Three equilibrium equations plus the hinge condition make it determinate.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: RL of a benchmark is 95.5 m. With back sight 1.2 m and fore sight 2.2 m, the RL of the next point is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Castigliano's first theorem gives:
questions.solution: The partial derivative of strain energy with respect to a load gives the deflection at that load.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A simply supported beam of span 5 m carries a central point load of 10 kN. The maximum bending moment is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Müller-Breslau principle is used to draw:
questions.solution: It states the influence line for a response equals the deflected shape from a unit displacement.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Kinematic indeterminacy of a structure equals the number of:
questions.solution: Kinematic indeterminacy = number of independent unknown joint displacements (degrees of freedom).

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If LL = 60% and PL = 25%, the plasticity index is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The method best suited for analysing trusses with a single redundant member is the:
questions.solution: Consistent deformation (unit load) handles internally redundant trusses efficiently.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: If E = 100 GPa and Poisson's ratio = 0.3, the modulus of rigidity G is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: In a perfect plane truss, the relation between members m and joints j is:
questions.solution: A statically determinate (perfect) plane truss satisfies m = 2j \u2212 3.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic compressive strength of M20 concrete is:
questions.solution: M20 denotes a characteristic cube strength of 20 MPa at 28 days.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The pressure at a depth of 5 m in water (\u03c1 = 1000 kg/m\u00b3, g = 9.81 m/s\u00b2) is about:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for concrete in the limit state method (IS 456) is:
questions.solution: \u03b3m for concrete is 1.5 in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A cantilever 2 m long carries a 8 kN load at its free end. The maximum bending moment is:
questions.solution: Max BM at fixed end = W \u00d7 L = 8 \u00d7 2 = 16 kNm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The partial safety factor for steel in the limit state method (IS 456) is:
questions.solution: \u03b3m for reinforcing steel is 1.15 in limit state design.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the minimum grade of concrete for reinforced concrete work is:
questions.solution: IS 456 specifies M20 as the minimum grade for RCC.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The Rankine active earth pressure coefficient Ka for a soil with \u03c6 = 36\u00b0 is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: As per IS 456, the maximum spacing of main bars in a slab is the smaller of:
questions.solution: Maximum spacing of main reinforcement is 3 times effective depth or 300 mm.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a simply supported beam with a central point load of 20 kN, the maximum shear force is:
questions.solution: Each reaction = W/2 = 10 kN, which is the maximum shear force.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The development length Ld of a bar is given by:
questions.solution: Ld = \u03c6\u03c3s/(4\u03c4bd), where \u03c6 is bar diameter and \u03c4bd is design bond stress.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Compression steel in a doubly reinforced beam is provided when:
options: Mu exceeds the limiting moment Mu,lim
questions.solution: When the design moment exceeds the section's balanced (limiting) capacity, compression steel is added.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The cross-sectional area of one 20 mm diameter bar is approximately:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The characteristic yield strength of Fe415 reinforcing steel is:
questions.solution: Fe415 denotes a yield strength of 415 MPa.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: For a void ratio e = 0.5, the porosity n is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: Lateral ties in an RCC column primarily prevent:
questions.solution: Ties hold the main bars in position and prevent their lateral buckling.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The limit state method of design checks both:
questions.solution: Limit state design verifies the limit states of collapse (strength) and serviceability.

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: A beam has overall depth 450 mm, clear cover 20 mm and 25 mm main bars. The effective depth is:

questions.subject: Paper-II · Professional Knowledge
questions.stem: The modular ratio m in working stress design is taken as:
questions.solution: IS 456 gives the modular ratio as m = 280/(3\u03c3cbc).
